title: 259 Mathematics NOTE
subject: Mathematics
objective: To form a cuboid and obtain a formula for its surface area
materials:
Cardboard, cellotape, cutter, ruler, sketch pen,
pencil, white paper, chart paper.
procedure:
1.   Take a cardboard of convenient size and paste a white paper on it.
2.   Make a shape involving two identical rectangles of dimensions   a   units
×   b   units, two identical rectangles of dimensions   b   units   ×   c   units and
two identical rectangles of dimensions   c   units   ×   a   units, using a thick
chart paper as shown in Fig. 1.
3.   Fold the six rectangles along the dotted lines to obtain a solid as shown
in Fig. 2.

Therefore, the surface area of the cube = 6 a 2 .
discussion:
This result is useful in estimating materials required for making cubical
boxes needed for packing.
Shape in Fig. 1 is called a   net   of the cube.
